name: State Persistence Deserialization
summary: Agentic frameworks that persist agent state (checkpoints, caches, stores) use unsafe deserialization by default — including pickle fallbacks, msgpack object reconstruction, and JSON constructor modes — enabling arbitrary code execution when the persistence layer is compromised.
mechanism: Agent state serializers default to maximum flexibility: pickle.loads() for binary fallback, msgpack ext types for object reconstruction, and JSON constructor mode for custom class instantiation. An attacker with write access to the persistence backend (database, Redis, filesystem) injects a crafted payload that executes arbitrary code when the checkpoint is loaded.
blast_radius: Arbitrary code execution in the agent runtime. Escalation from 'persistence layer write access' to full application compromise, including environment variable exfiltration and lateral movement.
prerequisite: Agent with persistent state (checkpointer, cache, or store). Attacker with write access to the persistence backend.

evidence.key_value: pickle.loads() on cache entries with pickle_fallback=True
Arbitrary object reconstruction during checkpoint load

defences.name: Strict Serialization Mode
defences.description: Serialize only explicitly allowed types via schema-derived allowlists. Set LANGGRAPH_STRICT_MSGPACK=true. Default to pickle_fallback=False.

defences.name: Persistence Layer Integrity
defences.description: Cryptographic integrity tags on serialized state. Restrict write access to persistence backends. Rotate credentials on suspected compromise.

defences.name: Safe-by-Default Serialization
defences.description: Use allowlist-only serialization as the default, requiring explicit opt-out for unsafe modes

poc.author: joernchen (original reporter for CVE-2025-64439)
poc.description: Inject Unicode surrogate to trigger JSON fallback → inject lc:2 constructor payload → os.system() executes on checkpoint load

environment.notes: Patched in langgraph-checkpoint >=3.0 (JSON constructor), >=4.0 (pickle fallback), langgraph >=1.0.10 (msgpack)

poc.notes: Three distinct deserialization vectors in the same serializer (JsonPlusSerializer)

events.notes: All three vectors patched across checkpoint >=3.0, >=4.0, langgraph >=1.0.10
